title: Kalopanax ricinifolius
intro: Kalopanax ricinifolius, also known as the Akebi tree or Eleuthero of Asia, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote health and well-being, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and adaptogenic properties that help to reduce stress and improve overall health. The bioactive constituents of Kalopanax ricinifolius include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fatigue, anxiety, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Kalopanax ricinifolius is a species of plant in the family Araliaceae. It belongs to the genus Kalopanax, which is a group of flowering plants native to East Asia. Kalopanax ricinifolius is also known as the giant spurge or Angelica tree due to its unique characteristics. The classification of Kalopanax ricinifolius places it within the kingdom Plantae. Its classification is further divided into more specific categories within the plant kingdom.

side_effects: Kalopanax ricinifolius is known to cause several side effects due to its toxicity. The plant contains ricin, a toxic protein that can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea if ingested. In severe cases, exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can lead to cardiovascular and respiratory problems. Long-term exposure to Kalopanax ricinifolius may also cause liver and kidney damage. However, the severity of the side effects often depends on the amount and method of exposure to the plant.
